gold_change_reason: The seed solution assumed only a single-character separator and exactly two fields. Scaled operators require support for non-default separators, extra fields, multi-character separators, and leading/trailing separators, all of which are handled by using str.split with the given separator and taking the last two elements.
covered_requirements: Handle read IDs with non-default separators
Handle read IDs with more than two fields
Handle read IDs with leading and trailing separators
implementation_notes: The function uses str.split with the provided separator, which naturally handles multi-character separators and retains empty parts for leading/trailing separators.
It extracts the last two parts after splitting, ensuring correct UMI (last) and cell barcode (second-to-last) even with extra fields.
If the read_id contains fewer than two separators (i.e., fewer than three parts), a ValueError is raised.
